_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L5 'Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L6 'Veteran Sergeant' variant (Tier 2 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Veteran Sergeant beat = 5 visible adds (full breastplate extension covering entire abdomen down to waist belt, steel tassets at hips with slight outward flare, layered 2-disc pauldrons bumping shoulder volume per silhouette mightiness rule, steel sabatons over leather boots, iron reinforcement strip running length of mace haft). Within-tier chained input strategy. Livery transition at L6: chest livery is now mostly covered by the extended breastplate; livery is visible only as a narrow SKIRT BAND between the wide leather belt (top edge) and the new tassets (top edge) — approximately 2-3 inches of red+mustard split tunic skirt visible. Audit notes: L5 keeper has the bevor merged into the breastplate as a single continuous upper-chest piece (rather than a separate bevor seam) — acceptable; prompt does not try to re-establish a separate bevor seam. L4 mace head iron banding rings: only ~2 of 3 visible (carried forward soft fail); prompt re-asserts iron banding rings present on the wooden mace head and adds the new haft reinforcement strip.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — TASSETS revised from many small narrow lames to 2 WIDER rectangular plates per AD detail-economy ('rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); SABATONS reduced to ANKLE PLATES ONLY at L6 per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier' (full continuous sabatons moved to L8). Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION: removed knife-sheath line (dropped per AD); added L3 steel skullcap line + L4 iron toe-cap line + L5 full bicep-to-wrist scale mail line. L5 mace head reinforcement plate removed from preserve (no longer in chain per AD detail-economy). Negatives updated to lock out narrow multi-lame tassets and full sabatons / shin greaves at L6.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v5 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L2 rim-rivet PRESERVE/negative refs — L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped; downstream shield-rim language no longer references L2 rivets.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.title: FULL BREASTPLATE EXTENSION DOWNWARD TO THE WAIST BELT
adds.summary: extend the existing L5 partial steel breastplate downward to cover the entire abdomen all the way to the wide leather belt.

adds.title: STEEL TASSETS HANGING FROM THE BELT AT BOTH HIPS
adds.summary: add TWO WIDER RECTANGULAR steel plates per hip, one wider plate above and one wider plate below, hanging from the wide leather belt at each hip.

adds.title: LAYERED 2-DISC PAULDRONS
adds.summary: add a smaller second disc layered ON TOP of each existing steel pauldron cap, riveted to the original.

adds.title: STEEL ANKLE PLATES OVER THE LEATHER BOOTS
adds.summary: add a cool silver-gray steel ankle band wrapping each boot at the ankle just above the boot's folded cuff, approximately 2 inches tall, articulated where the boot bends (subtle horizontal segment line so it reads as flexible plate).

adds.title: IRON REINFORCEMENT STRIP ON THE MACE HAFT
adds.summary: add a single thin flat iron strip running along the length of the wooden mace haft from the bulbous head down to the upper edge of the existing leather grip cord.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L5/composite/infantry_L5_v4_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every + + + feature shown in the input image: (a) the existing PARTIAL STEEL BREASTPLATE on the upper chest (the new step EXTENDS this breastplate downward — it does not replace it; the existing upper-chest steel reads as a continuous piece with the bevor at this level); (b) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (c) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT) — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath, NO second pouch; the new tassets cover the upper thigh BELOW the belt; the hip pouch hangs from the belt at the waist and remains visible at the upper edge of the tasset on that side; (d) the existing STEEL SKULLCAP visible UNDER the mail coif (small cool-gray steel dome at the very top of the head + thin steel rim band at the brow line + iron rivets at the temples) — preserved at this level; (e) TWO STEEL PAULDRON CAPS (the new step ADDS a smaller second disc on top of each existing pauldron); (f) STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms wrapping wrist to mid-forearm sitting ON TOP of the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves; (g) LEATHER GRIP CORD wrapping the upper third of the mace haft; (h) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the steel-faced shield; (i) STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with continuous dark-iron rim band, and the central cool-gray steel boss; (j) the IRON BANDING RINGS on the wooden mace head (between and around the spike rows — these RINGS REMAIN PRESENT on the wooden mace head, do not remove them) — NO reinforcement plate (removed 2026-05-07); (k) the FULL BICEP-TO-WRIST SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES on both arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales matching the mail coif) — sleeves cover the entire arm from shoulder seam to wrist with the steel vambraces overlaying wrist-to-mid-forearm; NO exposed bicep / elbow / upper-forearm gap; (l) the existing IRON TOE-CAP STRIP on the front of each boot — preserved; the new ankle plates per Edit Goal item 4 add to this; full continuous sabatons arrive at this level. DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the top of Tier 2 (Veteran Sergeant). The unit's silhouette becomes visibly bulkier — the breastplate fills the abdomen, layered pauldrons broaden the shoulders, tassets flare at the hips, and steel sabatons articulate the foot. The livery is now reduced to a narrow skirt band at the waist. (1) FULL BREASTPLATE EXTENSION DOWNWARD TO THE WAIST BELT — extend the existing partial steel breastplate downward to cover the entire abdomen all the way to the wide leather belt. The result is a single continuous cool silver-gray polished steel breastplate covering the front of the torso from collarbone (where it joins the bevor at the throat) down to the belt line at the waist. The breastplate has a slight medial ridge running vertically down the center from collar to belt. The lower edge of the breastplate is a clean horizontal line that sits flush with the top edge of the wide leather belt. The livery tunic chest is now FULLY COVERED by the extended breastplate — the red+mustard split tunic is no longer visible above the belt at the chest. The livery only remains visible as a narrow SKIRT BAND BELOW the belt (per Edit Goal item 2 below). The breastplate is one continuous piece — no horizontal seam between upper and lower halves. (2) STEEL TASSETS HANGING FROM THE BELT AT BOTH HIPS (2 WIDER LAMES per tasset, NOT many narrow lames — 'rely on shapes and silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree') — add TWO WIDER RECTANGULAR steel plates per hip, one wider plate above and one wider plate below, hanging from the wide leather belt at each hip. Each lame is approximately 6 inches wide and 4 inches tall (so each tasset is 2 wider lames stacked vertically, total ~6 inches tall × 6 inches wide), cool silver-gray polished steel matching the breastplate, with a slight outward flare at the lower edge so the bottom corners are wider than the top corners. The tassets cover the upper thigh below the belt. The two tassets are separated at the front by a vertical gap of approximately 3 inches — through this gap the LIVERY TUNIC SKIRT remains visible as a narrow band of red+mustard split cloth between the belt (top edge) and the lower edge of the tassets (the front gap shows the red+mustard skirt extending down to mid-thigh below the tassets). Each tasset's lames have two visible dark-iron rivets at the top edge of the upper lame where it rivets to a hidden leather strap that loops over the belt, and a thin shadow seam between the two stacked lames suggesting articulation. CRITICAL: each tasset is exactly 2 wider lames — NOT 3, NOT 4, NOT many narrow lames. Wider, fewer lames. The existing brown leather hip pouch (anatomical-RIGHT, the ONLY belt accessory) hangs from the belt at the waist and remains visible at the upper edge of the tasset on that side, sitting in front of the tasset (the pouch is not hidden by the tassets). NO knife / dagger sheath on the anatomical-LEFT hip. (3) LAYERED 2-DISC PAULDRONS (silhouette mightiness rule — visibly broader shoulder line than at this level) — add a smaller second disc layered ON TOP of each existing steel pauldron cap, riveted to the original. Each pauldron now reads as two stacked discs: the existing base disc visible in the input image (~5 inches diameter) and a smaller upper disc (~3.5 inches diameter) sitting on top of it like a stacked pauldron. Both discs are cool silver-gray polished steel; the upper disc has two visible dark-iron rivets at its lower seam where it joins the base disc. The shoulder envelope reads visibly broader than at this level — the silhouette must clearly increase in mass at the shoulder line. (4) STEEL ANKLE PLATES OVER THE LEATHER BOOTS — add a cool silver-gray steel ankle band wrapping each boot at the ankle just above the boot's folded cuff, approximately 2 inches tall, articulated where the boot bends (subtle horizontal segment line so it reads as flexible plate). Each ankle plate has two visible dark-iron rivets at the seam where it meets the leather. The existing iron toe-cap strip on the FRONT of each boot remains. CRITICAL: at the boot armor is ONLY the existing iron toe-cap + the new steel ankle plate — there is NO continuous steel covering the top of the foot between toe-cap and ankle (full toe+foot+ankle continuous sabatons arrive at this level). The brown leather body of the boot is clearly visible at the calf, the heel, AND across the central top of the foot between the toe-cap and the ankle plate. (5) IRON REINFORCEMENT STRIP ON THE MACE HAFT — add a single thin flat iron strip running along the length of the wooden mace haft from the bulbous head down to the upper edge of the existing leather grip cord. The strip is approximately half an inch wide, dark cool-gray iron, riveted at three points along its length (top near the mace head, middle, and bottom near the grip cord). The wooden haft remains warm-brown wood VISIBLE on either side of the iron strip; the strip is a narrow vertical reinforcement, NOT a wrap covering the entire haft. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (still framed at the bottom by the existing bevor / upper-chest steel; beard remains visible above the bevor's top edge) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the mail coif covering scalp, ears, and back-of-neck drape (front-of-throat covered by the existing bevor merged with the breastplate); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle (visible BETWEEN the new tassets at the front and through the gap); the existing brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip (visible at the upper edge of the right tasset) — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; the existing steel skullcap visible under the mail coif (small cool-gray dome at the top of the head + thin steel rim band at the brow line + iron rivets at temples); the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves on both arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales, no exposed bicep gap, vambraces sitting on top at the forearm); the existing leather grip cord wrapping the upper third of the mace haft (the new iron reinforcement strip per Edit Goal item 5 ends at the upper edge of this grip cord); the iron banding rings on the wooden mace head — these rings REMAIN PRESENT, do not remove; the spiked wooden mace held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — wooden haft, wooden bulbous mace head with iron banding plus new haft reinforcement strip, six iron spikes; the steel-faced round shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm with cross strap-bands, rim band and central steel boss — same round shape, same material, same features; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers (now visible at the calf below the new sabatons and between the tassets and the boots); the warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff at the calf level (now with new sabaton toe caps and ankle straps over them per Edit Goal item 4). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the upper haft, mace head dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of mail coif at ~16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the LIVERY skirt band fully — the red+mustard split tunic skirt MUST remain visible as a narrow band between the belt (top) and the tasset bottom edge through the front gap between the two tassets; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing or remove it entirely; DO NOT replace the livery tunic with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or any non-livery cloth; DO NOT extend the breastplate below the belt — the breastplate ends at the belt line at this level; DO NOT make the breastplate two pieces with a horizontal seam — single continuous piece from collar to belt; DO NOT make the tassets a single skirt-like fauld — they are TWO separate plates per hip with a visible vertical gap at the front; DO NOT make the tassets longer than ~6 inches at this level — extending tassets to mid-thigh arrives at this level; DO NOT make each tasset more than 2 wider lames — exactly 2 wider rectangular lames per tasset at this level (NOT 3 narrow lames, NOT 4, NOT many small lames 'rely on shapes and silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); DO NOT make the lames thin / narrow / many — the lames are wider plates, fewer of them; DO NOT add full continuous toe+foot+ankle sabatons at this level — boot armor at is the existing iron toe-cap + new ankle plate ONLY, with brown leather visible across the central top of the foot between them (full sabatons arrive at this level); DO NOT add shin greaves, knee plates, or any leg armor above the ankle plate at this level; DO NOT add a steel reinforcement plate or extra band wrapping the wooden mace head — the existing reinforcement plate was removed 2026-05-07; the existing iron banding rings are the only mace-head metal accents through; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, scabbard, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch is the ONLY belt accessory; DO NOT shrink the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves back to mid-bicep — sleeves remain full-length covering the entire arm with vambraces overlaying the wrist-to-mid-forearm section; DO NOT make the layered pauldrons three or four discs — exactly TWO stacked discs at this level (3-lame pauldrons arrive at this level, 4-lame at this level); DO NOT add gold rivets to the pauldrons — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add a third disc to the pauldrons; DO NOT make the sabatons cover the entire boot — the boots remain warm-brown leather visible at the calf and the heel and the central foot between toe cap and ankle strap; DO NOT extend sabatons up the calf to greaves — leg armor stays at the foot level at this level; DO NOT add greaves, knee plates, or any leg armor above the ankle strap; DO NOT replace the wooden mace head with a flanged steel head — that arrives at this level; DO NOT change the iron spike count or arrangement on the mace head; DO NOT remove the existing iron banding rings or the existing reinforcement plate; DO NOT make the haft reinforcement strip wrap the entire haft — it is a narrow vertical strip with wood visible on either side; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT add embossed sun-burst boss or detailed boss pattern — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif (introduces the open-face sallet); DO NOT add a closed visor, half-visor, or any face cover — face remains fully visible; DO NOT add gauntlets — gauntlets arrive at this level; DO NOT make the steel vambraces extend into gauntlets — vambraces stop at the wrist, hands remain bare; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the hair, the coif color, the trouser color, or the boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
